title: Oxine N-Oxide as an Analytical Reagent for the Colorimetric Estimation of Cerium (IV) and its Comparison with Oxine as a Chelating Agent
author: A. N. Bhat
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Oxine  N- oxide has been investigated as an analytical reagent for the colorimetric estimation of cerium (IV), which produces a deep brownish red, water-soluble complex with an ethanolic solution of the reagent. The complex is quite stable and the intensity of the colour remains unchanged between  p H 2.0 and 6.5. The complex obeys Lambert-Beer's  law at 420 m \(\mu\)  up to a dilution of 9 p.p.m. of cerium. The trivalent rare earths cerium, lanthanum and y cerium do not interfere in the above estimation.
steps_raw: Oxine  N- oxide has been investigated as an analytical reagent for the colorimetric estimation of cerium (IV), which produces a deep brownish red, water-soluble complex with an ethanolic solution of the reagent. The trivalent rare earths cerium, lanthanum and y cerium do not interfere in the above estimation.
